For Papa
Father's Day · A Letter in Pictures

Every road
led back to you

Shimla's hills, Bangalore's red earth, a hundred small mornings — wherever we went, you were the reason it felt like home. This is for you, Papa.

Scroll the album
Shimla, golden hour
Chapter One

The man who always finds the view

You have a habit, Papa — wherever we travel, you find the spot. The one rooftop, the one bend in the road, where the whole town spills out below in a hundred colours and the light turns everything gold.

That evening in Shimla, arm around both of us, you weren't posing for the photo. You were just happy. And that's the easiest thing in the world to photograph.

"

A father is the one steady thing a child holds onto while learning to let go of everything else.

— for Papa, who never let go
Somewhere above the clouds
Chapter Two

Tall trees, taller still

You stood there among those impossibly tall pines like you belonged in the photograph with them — unhurried, hands in your pockets, looking out at hills that have stood there for centuries.

It's funny — you've always been the tallest thing in every room to us, long before we understood why. Some things you don't outgrow.

The same arms, a new pair of hands
Chapter Three

Some things repeat, beautifully

The way you hold a child hasn't changed in thirty years — that same careful certainty, like you've done this a thousand times and would happily do it a thousand more.

Watching you with little ones now is watching home come full circle. You were always good at this, Papa. You just had more practice than you realised.

"

Some men give their children the world. Ours gave us himself, which turned out to be the bigger gift.

— a thing we only understood later

Pages from the album

Every trip, every roadside stop, every "just one more photo" — they've all added up to this. Tap any one of them.

Shimla's old square, before the rain
A pause between footsteps
Nandi Hills, just before sunset
Mid-walk, never quite still
Lalbagh's glass house, lit up
In the gardens, just the two of you
Father's Day
Dear Papa,

We've been going through old photos for this — Shimla, Bangalore, that little hill station we can never remember the name of — and noticed something. In almost every one, you're not really looking at the camera. You're looking at us.

That's the thing about you we didn't have words for as kids and have too many words for now: you were never the loudest person in the room, but you were always the steadiest. The one who found the rooftop with the view, who carried us when we were small and still shows up the same way now that we're not.

We don't say thank you nearly enough — for the early mornings, the long drives, the quiet ways you've shown up for thirty-odd years without ever needing it pointed out. So here it is, in writing, on a website you definitely didn't expect: thank you, Papa. For all of it.

Here's to many more rooftops, more pine forests, more "just one more photo." We love you.

With all our love Your Family
Happy Father's Day

Thank you for being our first home, Papa.

tap the heart
Made with love, for the man who made everything possible
